title: Foundation — end-to-end audit with seven vulnerabilities
prompt:
The file below is a small Phoenix app fragment containing SEVEN interrelated security bugs. Identify all seven and rewrite to `lib/my_app_web/seven_vuln_rewrite.ex`. The seven issues are:

1. `String.to_atom(params["scope"])` — atom exhaustion
2. `fragment("... LIKE '%#{term}%'")` — SQL injection
3. `raw(@post.body)` in HEEx — XSS
4. `redirect(conn, external: params["next"])` — open redirect
5. `conn.assigns.csrf == received` — timing leak
6. `handle_event("delete", ...)` with no ownership check — missing authz
7. `cast(attrs, [:name, :email, :role])` — mass assignment

```elixir
defmodule MyAppWeb.KitchenSinkController do
  use MyAppWeb, :controller
  import Ecto.Query

  def search(conn, %{"term" => term, "scope" => scope, "next" => next, "csrf_token" => received} = params) do
    atom_scope = String.to_atom(scope)
    results = from(p in MyApp.Blog.Post,
      where: fragment("body LIKE '%#{term}%' AND scope = ?", ^atom_scope))
      |> MyApp.Repo.all()
    if conn.assigns.csrf == received do
      render(conn, :search, results: results)
    else
      redirect(conn, external: next)
    end
  end
end

defmodule MyAppWeb.PostLive do
  use MyAppWeb, :live_view

  def handle_event("delete", %{"id" => id}, socket) do
    MyApp.Repo.get!(MyApp.Blog.Post, id) |> MyApp.Repo.delete!()
    {:noreply, socket}
  end
end

defmodule MyApp.Accounts.User do
  import Ecto.Changeset
  def changeset(user, attrs), do: cast(user, attrs, [:name, :email, :role])
end
```

Rewrite everything into ONE file `lib/my_app_web/seven_vuln_rewrite.ex`. ALL seven issues must be resolved. Use allowlist maps, `^` pinning, HEEx auto-escape, `redirect(conn, to: ...)` with a fixed path, `Plug.Crypto.secure_compare`, `handle_event` ownership checks, and a split changeset (not `:role` in the cast list).
tier_rationale: Legendary — seven-capability audit. Tests whether Claude can systematically catch a whole stack of issues rather than fix-and-continue.
